decisions.title: Auth module shipped last Friday
decisions.detail: The authentication module was successfully shipped last Friday with no P0 bugs reported.

evidence.quote: Alex opened by confirming the auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.

decisions.title: JWT token expiry set to 24 hours
decisions.detail: The expiry for JWT tokens is configured for 24 hours, based on an agreement made in Sprint 2.

evidence.quote: Ben said the JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours as agreed in Sprint 2.

decisions.title: Password reset emails are not rate-limited
decisions.detail: Carol flagged a current issue where password reset emails are not subject to rate limiting.

evidence.quote: Carol flagged that password reset emails are not being rate-limited.

decisions.title: Maximum 3 password reset attempts per hour
decisions.detail: It was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28 that users should be limited to a maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour.

evidence.quote: Max 3 reset attempts per hour was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28.
Password reset rate limit: 3 per hour

decisions.title: API gateway will use AWS API Gateway
decisions.detail: The API gateway implementation will utilize AWS API Gateway.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with a 100 req/min limit per user.
API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: API gateway limit of 100 req/min per user
decisions.detail: The API gateway will enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user, based on Ben's recommendation from load tests.

evidence.quote: The API gateway will use AWS API Gateway with a 100 req/min limit per user.
API gateway: AWS API Gateway, 100 req/min per user cap

decisions.title: Mobile app team blocked by auth API spec publication
decisions.detail: The mobile application development team is currently blocked awaiting the publication of the authentication API specification.

evidence.quote: Alex noted the mobile app team is blocked until the auth API spec is published.

decisions.title: Target publish date for auth API spec is 2026-07-05
decisions.detail: The target date for publishing the authentication API specification is July 5, 2026.

decisions.title: Automated integration tests for auth endpoints
decisions.detail: All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests in place before Sprint 3 sign-off.

evidence.quote: Carol wants all three auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) to have automated integration tests before Sprint 3 sign-off.

decisions.title: Fallback behaviour if API gateway goes down
decisions.detail: An open question remains regarding the system's fallback behaviour if the API gateway becomes unavailable.

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Refresh token invalidation on password reset
decisions.detail: It needs to be clarified whether refresh tokens are invalidated automatically when a user performs a password reset.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement and configure rate limiting for password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 per hour.
Configure AWS API Gateway to enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
Develop and publish the authentication API specification.
Implement automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and password reset authentication endpoints.

constraints.text: Password reset attempts must be limited to a maximum of 3 per hour.
The API gateway must enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The authentication API specification must be published by 2026-07-05, as the mobile app team is blocked.
Automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints must be in place before Sprint 3 sign-off.

architectural_decisions.text: JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours.
The API gateway implementation will utilize AWS API Gateway.

open_technical_questions: What is the required fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable?
Should refresh tokens be invalidated automatically when a user performs a password reset?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user successfully logs in, When the user's JWT token is used within 24 hours of issuance, Then the API request should be successfully authorized.
Given a user successfully logs in, When the user's JWT token is used after 24 hours of issuance, Then the API request should be rejected due to an expired token.

Given a user attempts to reset their password, When the user performs 3 password reset attempts within a 1-hour window, Then all 3 attempts should be successful, and a reset email should be sent for each valid request.

Given a user attempts to reset their password, When the user attempts a 4th password reset within the same 1-hour window, Then the attempt should be rejected with an appropriate rate limit error message, and no email should be sent.
Given a user makes API requests through the API Gateway, When the user makes 100 requests within a 1-minute window, Then all 100 requests should be successfully processed.
Given a user makes API requests through the API Gateway, When the user makes a 101st request within the same 1-minute window, Then the request should be rejected with a rate limit error.
Automated integration tests for the 'login', 'refresh', and 'reset' authentication endpoints must be present and passing as part of the CI/CD pipeline.
test_scenarios: Verify JWT token usage: Log in successfully. Wait 23 hours and 59 minutes. Attempt an authenticated API request with the token. Assert success. Wait an additional 2 minutes (total 24 hours 1 minute). Attempt another authenticated API request with the same token. Assert token expiry error.
Verify password reset rate limit (happy path): Request a password reset 3 times within a 30-minute period. Verify 3 successful responses and 3 password reset emails received.

Verify password reset rate limit (failure path): Request a password reset 3 times within a 15-minute period. Immediately attempt a 4th password reset. Verify the 4th request is blocked and no email is sent.

Verify password reset rate limit (reset counter): Request a password reset 3 times within a 30-minute period. Wait 1 hour and 5 minutes after the first request. Attempt another password reset. Verify this request is successful.
Verify API gateway rate limit (happy path): Send 99 valid API requests for a single user within 50 seconds. Assert all requests return successful responses.

Verify API gateway rate limit (failure path): Send 101 valid API requests for a single user within 5 seconds. Assert the first 100 requests are successful and the 101st request (and subsequent requests within that minute) receives a rate limit error.

Verify automated integration tests: Check the CI/CD pipeline execution results to confirm that automated integration tests for 'login', 'refresh', and 'reset' endpoints have run and passed successfully before Sprint 3 sign-off.
edge_cases: JWT token expiry: Attempt to use a JWT token at exactly 24 hours from its issuance time.
Password reset rate limit: Submit 3 password reset requests concurrently for the same user.
Password reset rate limit: Attempt password resets from different IP addresses for the same user within the 1-hour window.
API gateway rate limit: Send 100 requests in a rapid burst (e.g., within 1 second) for a single user.
API gateway rate limit: Test a single user exceeding the rate limit, then sending requests from a different device/IP for the same user.
API gateway rate limit: Test multiple distinct users from the same IP address hitting their individual rate limits.
Authentication fields: Test authentication endpoints with empty, excessively long, or invalid characters for username/email and password fields.
unresolved_behaviors: What is the system's defined fallback behavior if the API gateway becomes unavailable? (e.g., specific error messages, redirects, service degradation modes)
Are refresh tokens automatically invalidated when a user performs a password reset? If so, what is the expected user experience (e.g., force re-login)?

decision_history: The JWT token expiry is configured for 24 hours, based on an agreement made in Sprint 2.
It was agreed in the security review on 2026-06-28 that users should be limited to a maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour.
The API gateway implementation will utilize AWS API Gateway.
The API gateway will enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user, based on Ben's recommendation from load tests.
All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests in place before Sprint 3 sign-off.
dependencies: The mobile application development team is currently blocked awaiting the publication of the authentication API specification.
risks: Password reset emails are currently not subject to rate limiting, which is a significant security vulnerability (High Impact, High Likelihood if unaddressed).

Lack of defined fallback behaviour if the API gateway becomes unavailable could lead to service disruption and poor user experience (Inferred from D010; High Impact, Medium Likelihood if not proactively addressed).
open_questions: Are refresh tokens automatically invalidated when a user performs a password reset? (Requires clarification on system behavior and acceptance criteria.)
milestones: Authentication module was successfully shipped last Friday with no P0 bugs reported.
Auth API spec is targeted to be published by 2026-07-05.
Automated integration tests for all three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must be in place before Sprint 3 sign-off.

system_requirements: The system SHALL configure JWT tokens to expire after 24 hours.
The system SHALL limit users to a maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour.
The API Gateway SHALL enforce a rate limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The system's authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) MUST have automated integration tests in place before Sprint 3 sign-off.
data_flows: User authentication requests flow to the Authentication module, which issues JWT tokens.
Password reset requests flow to a system component responsible for enforcing rate limits and initiating email dispatch.
Email dispatch system sends password reset emails to users.
All external client requests to backend services flow through the AWS API Gateway.
Mobile application communicates with the Authentication API.
interface_contracts: Authentication API Specification: Defines endpoints for login, token refresh, and password reset (inferred — needs confirmation).
Authentication API: Login Endpoint (inferred — needs confirmation).
Authentication API: Refresh Endpoint (inferred — needs confirmation).
Authentication API: Password Reset Endpoint (inferred — needs confirmation).

constraints.text: The authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05.
The publication of the authentication API specification is a critical dependency for the mobile application development team, currently blocking their progress.

architectural_decisions.text: The API gateway implementation SHALL utilize AWS API Gateway.

open_analysis_questions: What is the defined fallback behavior and system state if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable?
Clarify the system's behavior regarding refresh token invalidation upon a user performing a password reset.

stakeholder.business_summary: The core user authentication system has successfully launched without critical issues. However, our mobile app development is currently paused as it awaits the final details for integrating with this new system, which are expected by July 5th. Key security measures around password resets and overall system stability are still being addressed.
key_impact: The main user login and account system is now operational, enabling further development.
There is a current security vulnerability where password reset emails can be sent repeatedly without limits, which needs to be fixed to prevent misuse.
Mobile application development is currently delayed until the detailed plan for connecting to the new user authentication system is published.
New security measures will limit users to three password reset attempts per hour, improving account protection.
The system's capacity will be managed to prevent overload, ensuring a stable experience for users.
major_unresolved_questions: What happens if the main system managing all incoming requests (API gateway) unexpectedly stops working? We need a plan to ensure continuous service.
When a user resets their password, are all their existing login sessions immediately ended to prevent unauthorized access? This is important for security.

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions. No hallucinations, quality issues, prompt injection attempts, or sensitive content were found. The outputs are well-structured and tailored for their respective audiences.

evidence_quotes: Alex opened by confirming the auth module shipped last Friday with zero P0 bugs.
